compiled_state.title: As a DSO orchestrator, merge conflicts are detected and escalated
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: When git merge of worktree branch into session branch fails due to conflict, detect and escalate gracefully
**Why**: With multiple worktrees merging sequentially, one conflict should not block all other worktrees
**Scope**:
- IN: Conflict detection at merge time, ticket comment creation, re-implementation queue, non-conflicting worktrees continue
- OUT: Automatic conflict resolution, Clash proactive prevention (Epic B)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a merge conflict creates a ticket comment "CONFLICT: worktree <id> blocked" on the active story
  ← Satisfies: "Merge conflicts flagged for re-implementation or user escalation"
- When this story is complete, non-conflicting worktrees continue merging after a conflict is detected (conflict does not block the queue)
  ← Satisfies: "Merge conflicts flagged for re-implementation or user escalation"
- When this story is complete, conflicting worktree is queued for re-implementation after all non-conflicting merges complete (serialized re-implementation against post-merge session state)
  ← Satisfies: "Merge conflicts flagged for re-implementation or user escalation"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing including a controlled git conflict injection test
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Testing] Need controlled setup producing a real git conflict for testing
- [Reliability] Re-implementation must be serialized AFTER all non-conflicting merges complete — re-implementing against the pre-merge state could cascade into new conflicts
- [Reliability] Non-conflicting worktrees must not be blocked by one conflicting worktree

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.

comments.body: Story complete: conflict detection and escalation implemented. 21 test assertions pass.
comments.author: Test
